summary: Cleaned Popo and Nana motion-state table definitions by replacing repeated raw motion-flag and move-id hex constants with named ftPopo motion flag enum values and FtMoveId expressions. The change added reusable ftPopo_MotionFlags definitions in ftPopo/forward.h, included that header from Nana init so Nana can share Popo special-move flags, and left an explicit todo for a misnamed state that appears to belong to SpecialHi. No PR body or review discussion was available.

key_files.reason: Added ftPopo_MotionFlags enum and included ft/forward.h so motion-state flags can be built from FtStateChange_* names.
Replaced raw motion flag and move-id constants in Popo's MotionState table with named ftPp_MF_* flags and FtMoveId_* expressions.
Included ftPopo/forward.h and applied the same named motion flag and move-id cleanup to Nana's MotionState table.

what_changed: Added typedef enum ftPopo_MotionFlags in ftPopo/forward.h with named composites such as ftPp_MF_Special, ftPp_MF_SpecialN, ftPp_MF_SpecialAirN, ftPp_MF_SpecialS, ftPp_MF_SpecialAirS, ftPp_MF_SpecialHi, ftPp_MF_SpecialAirHi, ftPp_MF_SpecialLw, and ftPp_MF_MS_358.

Replaced raw state-change flag constants such as 0x00340111, 0x00340511, 0x00340212, 0x00340612, 0x00340213, 0x00340613, 0x00340014, and 0x00340414 in Popo and Nana motion tables with named ftPp_MF_* values.

Replaced raw move-id bitfield constants such as 0x12800000, 0x13800000, 0x14000000, and 0x15800000 with expressions like (FtMoveId_SpecialN << 24) | (1 << 23), (FtMoveId_SpecialS << 24) | (1 << 23), FtMoveId_SpecialHi << 24, and (FtMoveId_SpecialLw << 24) | (1 << 23).
Made Nana reuse Popo's special-move motion flag definitions by adding #include "ftPopo/forward.h" to ftNn_Init.c.
Added #include "forward.h" and #include "ft/forward.h" to ftPp_Init.c to make the named flags and FtMoveId/FtStateChange definitions available.
Added an inline todo above motion state 310: "Fix names, this is part of SpecialHi", documenting that the current SpecialLw function names for that table entry are likely misleading.
Changed three item setup calls in ftPp_Init_OnLoad from unsigned hex constants 0x6AU, 0x6BU, and 0x71U to decimal constants 106, 107, and 113.
Updated embedded assembly formatting in ftPp_SpecialAirN_Coll to use decimal immediates/offsets in some instructions while preserving the disassembly comments.
smart_moves: Factored repeated opaque hex flags into a small per-fighter enum instead of expanding every table row inline, making the intent of each motion state visible without changing table shape.
Defined air variants by composing ground variants with FtStateChange_SkipUpdateParasol, which captures the relationship between grounded and aerial Popo special states.
Shared Popo motion flag definitions with Nana rather than duplicating equivalent constants, matching the Ice Climbers' shared special-move implementation structure.
Used FtMoveId_* shift expressions for the second MotionState field, preserving exact bit patterns while documenting which special move the state belongs to.
Kept uncertain naming explicitly marked with a todo instead of inventing a rename for state 310 without stronger evidence.
decomp_lessons: MotionState table cleanup can be made safer by replacing raw constants with named bitwise compositions that evaluate to the same value.
When a fighter pair shares move implementation code, put shared motion flag constants in a forward header that both init files can include.
For move-id fields, prefer expressions based on FtMoveId_* and explicit high-bit masks over unexplained 0x12/0x13/0x14-style packed constants.
If a function name appears inconsistent with the move-id/flags evidence, leave a localized todo rather than renaming across the codebase prematurely.
Small header cleanups can substantially improve searchability of motion tables without requiring functional decompilation of the state callbacks.
naming_conventions: Popo-specific motion flag enum is named ftPopo_MotionFlags.
Motion flag constants use the ftPp_MF_* prefix, parallel to Popo's ftPp_* symbol prefix.
Generic composite base flag is named ftPp_MF_Special.
Grounded special flags use names like ftPp_MF_SpecialN, ftPp_MF_SpecialS, ftPp_MF_SpecialHi, and ftPp_MF_SpecialLw.
Aerial special flags use names like ftPp_MF_SpecialAirN, ftPp_MF_SpecialAirS, and ftPp_MF_SpecialAirHi.
Unknown or not-yet-understood motion flags retain a numbered-state style name such as ftPp_MF_MS_358.
Move-id field expressions use FtMoveId_SpecialN, FtMoveId_SpecialS, FtMoveId_SpecialHi, and FtMoveId_SpecialLw.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The PR appears to preserve matching by replacing numeric table constants with equivalent enum and bit-shift expressions rather than changing table entries structurally.
Packed move-id constants were decoded into high-byte shifts plus an explicit (1 << 23) flag where present.
Composite motion flags were expressed from FtStateChange_* bit names, making equivalence to original hex constants auditable.
The embedded asm hunk in ftPp_SpecialAirN_Coll changed some stack sizes and offsets from hex notation to decimal notation, a formatting/readability cleanup rather than evidence of logic change.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only visible feedback-like note is an added source todo: "Fix names, this is part of SpecialHi" for motion state 310.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that resolve the todo for Popo/Nana motion state 310 being part of SpecialHi despite SpecialLw callback names.
Search for other fighter MotionState tables still using raw packed constants like 0x12800000 or 0x00340213.
Compare ftPopo_MotionFlags against other fighters' motion flag enums to standardize FtStateChange_* composition naming.
Investigate whether ftPp_MF_MS_358 later receives a semantic name.
